page: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dual Intel Xeon 8175M processors. It features 96 virtual CPUs (48 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 384 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled directly with 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also a key strength, showing strong read and write bandwidth in large-block uncached tests, alongside high Redis SET operations throughput at 76.56 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains average, as evidenced by middle-tier results in single-threaded stress-ng and Geekbench tests. LLM text generation performance is poor, particularly for text generation tasks, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high resource density, the m5d.metal offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory-to-CPU ratios makes this server suitable for large-scale databases, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications. It is less optimal for GPU-dependent tasks or workloads dominated by sequential LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server configured with 96 vCPUs (48 physical Intel Xeon 8175M cores) running at 3.1 GHz and 384 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and high Redis throughput, though single-core execution and LLM text generation are average to poor. This server is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and enterprise workloads requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal server combining high-capacity local NVMe storage with balanced multi-core compute and memory resources.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8175M processors running at 3.1 GHz
384 GB of DDR4 system memory providing a balanced 4 GB per core
3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis performance
Bare-metal deployment eliminating hypervisor overhead for demanding workloads
Best suited for databases, software compilation, and static web serving
